criteria_results.description: Exporting a project generates a `.zip` file containing `project.json`, `document.pdf`, and an `assets/` directory.
criteria_results.evidence: client/src/hooks/useExport.ts:22-26, 68-75 creates a JSZip instance, adds an 'assets' folder, 'project.json', 'document.pdf' (and 'overlay.pdf'), and triggers a download of 'project-bundle.zip'.

criteria_results.description: `project.json` inside the ZIP uses relative paths (e.g., 'assets/image_1.png') for image content instead of Base64.
criteria_results.evidence: client/src/hooks/useExport.ts:29-65 implements `processAsset` which saves assets to the ZIP's 'assets/' folder and returns a relative path like 'assets/img_...png', which replaces the original content in `finalObjects`.

criteria_results.description: Importing the generated ZIP file restores the full project state including the PDF and all visual objects.
criteria_results.evidence: client/src/hooks/useImport.ts:18-86 `handleZipImport` reads 'project.json', extracts 'document.pdf', and resolves all relative paths starting with 'assets/' back into usable Blob URLs before dispatching `IMPORT_PROJECT`.

criteria_results.description: Importing a folder (directory upload) containing the extracted ZIP content correctly resolves assets.
criteria_results.evidence: client/src/hooks/useImport.ts:88-144 `handleDirectoryImport` uses a `findFileByPath` helper to match relative paths from the JSON manifest against the uploaded FileList, supporting both direct matches and browser-prefixed paths.

criteria_results.description: Legacy `.json` files with embedded Base64 data can still be imported.
criteria_results.evidence: client/src/hooks/useImport.ts:160-166 directly handles .json files by parsing and dispatching without requiring asset resolution. Also, asset resolution in other handlers returns original content if it doesn't start with 'assets/'.

criteria_results.description: The PDF file is automatically loaded into the viewer when a ZIP project is imported.
criteria_results.evidence: client/src/hooks/useImport.ts:31-48, 77-85 extracts 'document.pdf' from the ZIP and includes it in the `IMPORT_PROJECT` payload, which `editorReducer` (client/src/lib/editor-context.tsx:142) merges into the global state.

suggestions: Consider revoking Blob URLs created during import when the project is closed or a new one is imported to prevent potential memory leaks in long-running sessions.
next_focus: The implementation is complete and meets all requirements. No further action needed.
